tasks.title: User avatars + profile management — full stack
tasks.description: Add user profile features: avatar, display name, timezone. Full vertical slice from DB to UI. **1. Prisma schema (`apps/api/prisma/schema/auth.prisma`):** Add to User model: ```prisma displayName String? @map("display_name") avatarUrl String? @map("avatar_url") timezone String? @default("UTC") ``` **2. Migration SQL:** ```sql ALTER TABLE "users" ADD COLUMN "display_name" TEXT; ALTER TABLE "users" ADD COLUMN "avatar_url" TEXT; ALTER TABLE "users" ADD COLUMN "timezone" TEXT DEFAULT 'UTC'; ``` **3. GraphQL typeDefs (`apps/api/src/graphql/typedefs/auth.ts`):** Add to User type: `displayName: String`, `avatarUrl: String`, `timezone: String`. Add new mutation: `updateProfile(displayName: String, avatarUrl: String, timezone: String): User!` **4. Auth resolver (`apps/api/src/graphql/resolvers/auth.ts`):** Add `updateProfile` mutation: - Require auth - Accept optional displayName, avatarUrl, timezone - Validate avatarUrl is a valid URL if provided (simple regex or URL constructor) - Validate timezone is a non-empty string if provided - Update user record, return updated user For avatars, support two approaches: - URL-based: user provides a URL (Gravatar, uploaded image, etc.) - Gravatar fallback: if no avatarUrl set, generate from email hash: `https://gravatar.com/avatar/${md5(email)}?d=identicon&s=80` Add a `User.avatarUrl` field resolver that returns the stored URL or Gravatar fallback. **5. Create UserAvatar component (`apps/web/src/components/shared/UserAvatar.tsx`):** ```typescript interface UserAvatarProps { email: string; avatarUrl?: string | null; displayName?: string | null; size?: 'sm' | 'md' | 'lg'; // 24px, 32px, 40px className?: string; } ``` - Show `` if avatarUrl exists, fall back to initials circle (first letter of displayName or email) - Colored background based on hash of email (deterministic color) - Tooltip showing displayName or email on hover **6. Create ProfilePage (`apps/web/src/pages/ProfilePage.tsx`):** - Route: `/app/profile` - Form fields: Display Name (text), Avatar URL (text + preview), Timezone (dropdown with common timezones) - Email (read-only, shown for reference) - Save button calls `updateProfile` mutation - Toast on success **7. Add route (`apps/web/src/App.tsx`):** Add `/app/profile` route rendering ProfilePage. **8. Update AppLayout (`apps/web/src/pages/AppLayout.tsx`):** - Add 'Profile' link in sidebar (user icon) - Show UserAvatar in sidebar header (replace or augment the current email display) **9. Integrate UserAvatar across the app:** - In `OrgSettings.tsx` team members list — show avatar next to email - In `TaskDetailPanel.tsx` assignee display — show avatar (this file is in the W1 set but a small addition of the component import is acceptable) - In `KanbanBoard.tsx` task cards — show small assignee avatar - In `BacklogView.tsx` task rows — show small assignee avatar - In `NotificationCenter.tsx` — show avatar next to notification For components you can't modify (not in your files list), just create and export UserAvatar — other workers or future tasks will integrate it. **Verification:** `npx prisma validate` passes, `pnpm typecheck` passes for both packages.
acceptanceCriteria: User model has displayName, avatarUrl, timezone fields
updateProfile mutation validates and persists profile fields
User.avatarUrl field resolver returns Gravatar fallback when no custom URL
UserAvatar component renders image or initials with deterministic color
ProfilePage with form for display name, avatar URL, timezone
/app/profile route registered
Profile link in sidebar, avatar in sidebar header
UserAvatar shown in OrgSettings team list
npx prisma validate passes
pnpm typecheck passes for both packages

tasks.title: Project-level roles + automation rules — full stack
tasks.description:
Add per-project access control and a configurable automation rules engine. Full vertical slice.

**1. Prisma schema — new models:**

Create `apps/api/prisma/schema/projectrole.prisma`:
```prisma
model ProjectMember {
  id        String @id @default(uuid())
  projectId String @map("project_id")
  userId    String @map("user_id")
  role      String @default("editor")  // viewer, editor, admin
  createdAt DateTime @default(now()) @map("created_at")

  project Project @relation(fields: [projectId], references: [projectId], onDelete: Cascade)
  user    User    @relation(fields: [userId], references: [userId], onDelete: Cascade)

  @@unique([projectId, userId])
  @@index([projectId])
  @@index([userId])
  @@map("project_members")
}

model AutomationRule {
  id          String  @id @default(uuid())
  projectId   String  @map("project_id")
  orgId       String  @map("org_id")
  name        String
  trigger     String  // JSON: { event: 'task.status_changed', condition: { newStatus: 'done' } }
  action      String  // JSON: { type: 'notify_assignee' } or { type: 'move_to_column', column: 'Done' }
  enabled     Boolean @default(true)
  createdAt   DateTime @default(now()) @map("created_at")

  project Project @relation(fields: [projectId], references: [projectId], onDelete: Cascade)
  org     Org     @relation(fields: [orgId], references: [orgId])

  @@index([projectId, enabled])
  @@map("automation_rules")
}
```

Add relations to existing models:
- `apps/api/prisma/schema/project.prisma`: add `members ProjectMember[]` and `automationRules AutomationRule[]`
- `apps/api/prisma/schema/auth.prisma`: add `projectMemberships ProjectMember[]`
- `apps/api/prisma/schema/org.prisma`: add `automationRules AutomationRule[]`

**2. Migration SQL** for both new tables.

**3. GraphQL typeDefs — create `apps/api/src/graphql/typedefs/projectrole.ts`:**
```graphql
type ProjectMember { id: ID!, userId: ID!, email: String!, role: String!, createdAt: String! }
type AutomationRule { id: ID!, name: String!, trigger: String!, action: String!, enabled: Boolean!, createdAt: String! }

# Queries
projectMembers(projectId: ID!): [ProjectMember!]!
automationRules(projectId: ID!): [AutomationRule!]!

# Mutations
addProjectMember(projectId: ID!, userId: ID!, role: String): ProjectMember!
removeProjectMember(projectId: ID!, userId: ID!): Boolean!
updateProjectMemberRole(projectId: ID!, userId: ID!, role: String!): ProjectMember!
createAutomationRule(projectId: ID!, name: String!, trigger: String!, action: String!): AutomationRule!
updateAutomationRule(ruleId: ID!, name: String, trigger: String, action: String, enabled: Boolean): AutomationRule!
deleteAutomationRule(ruleId: ID!): Boolean!
```

**4. Update schema.ts** to import and assemble the new typeDefs.

**5. Create resolver (`apps/api/src/graphql/resolvers/projectrole.ts`):**
- `projectMembers` query: fetch members with user email
- `addProjectMember`: require org:admin or project admin, upsert member
- `removeProjectMember`: require admin, prevent removing last admin
- `updateProjectMemberRole`: require admin, validate role enum
- `automationRules` query: fetch rules for project
- CRUD for automation rules: require project admin, validate trigger/action JSON structure with Zod

**6. Create automation engine (`apps/api/src/utils/automationEngine.ts`):**
```typescript
export async function executeAutomations(
  prisma: PrismaClient,
  event: { type: string; projectId: string; taskId?: string; data: Record<string, unknown> }
): Promise<void>
```
- Fetch enabled rules for the project matching the event type
- Parse trigger JSON, check conditions (e.g., newStatus === 'done')
- Execute matching actions:
  - `notify_assignee`: call createNotification
  - `move_to_column`: update task.sprintColumn
  - `set_status`: update task.status
  - `assign_to`: update task.assigneeId
- Fire-and-forget (don't block the mutation)

**7. Wire automation engine into task mutations (`apps/api/src/graphql/resolvers/task.ts`):**
After `updateTask` completes, call `executeAutomations(prisma, { type: 'task.status_changed', projectId, taskId, data: { oldStatus, newStatus } })`. Only call when status actually changed.

**8. Update resolver index (`apps/api/src/graphql/resolvers/index.ts`):**
Import and spread the new projectrole queries/mutations.

**9. Create ProjectSettingsModal (`apps/web/src/components/ProjectSettingsModal.tsx`):**
Two tabs:
- **Members tab:** List of project members with role dropdown (viewer/editor/admin), add member button (select from org users), remove button
- **Automation tab:** List of rules with name, trigger summary, action summary, enabled toggle. Add rule form with: name input, trigger type dropdown (status changed, assigned, due date passed), condition fields, action type dropdown (notify, move column, set status, assign), action fields. Delete button.

**10. Wire into ProjectDetail (`apps/web/src/pages/ProjectDetail.tsx`):**
Add a gear/settings icon button in the toolbar that opens ProjectSettingsModal.

**Verification:** `npx prisma validate` passes, `pnpm typecheck` passes for both packages.
acceptanceCriteria: ProjectMember model with projectId+userId unique constraint and role field
AutomationRule model with trigger/action JSON fields
CRUD mutations for project members and automation rules
Permission checks: only project/org admins can manage members and rules
Automation engine evaluates rules on task status changes
Actions supported: notify_assignee, move_to_column, set_status, assign_to
ProjectSettingsModal with Members and Automation tabs
Settings button in ProjectDetail toolbar
npx prisma validate passes
pnpm typecheck passes for both packages
tasks.reviewNotes: Typecheck fails: PrismaClient does not have projectMember or automationRule properties. Fix: 1) Create a migration SQL file for the project_members and automation_rules tables. 2) Run npx prisma generate so the Prisma client types include ProjectMember and AutomationRule. Also: auth.prisma now has displayName/avatarUrl/timezone and ProjectDetail.tsx now imports BugReportModal+PRDBreakdownModal from task-001/task-003 merges - rebase onto main before resubmitting.

tasks.title: Task dedup + bug report + PRD breakdown — AI features with full UI
tasks.description:
Implement three AI features end-to-end: task dedup in generation, bug report parsing, and PRD breakdown.

**FEATURE 1: Deduplicate task generation**

In `apps/api/src/ai/promptBuilder.ts`:
- Update `buildTaskPlanPrompt` to accept `existingTaskTitles?: string[]`
- If provided, add to prompt: 'IMPORTANT: Do NOT create tasks with the same or very similar titles as these existing tasks:\n{titles joined by newline, cap at 30}'

In `apps/api/src/ai/aiService.ts`:
- Add `existingTaskTitles?: string[]` param to `generateTaskPlan`
- Pass through to prompt builder

In `apps/api/src/graphql/resolvers/ai.ts`:
- In `generateTaskPlan` and `previewTaskPlan`: fetch existing task titles for the project (`prisma.task.findMany({ where: { projectId, archived: false }, select: { title: true } })`)
- Pass to service
- Post-generation dedup: filter out tasks whose title matches existing (case-insensitive trim)
- Apply same logic to `expandTask` with sibling titles

**FEATURE 2: Bug report → Task**

In `apps/api/src/ai/aiTypes.ts`:
```typescript
export const BugReportTaskSchema = z.object({
  title: z.string(),
  description: z.string().describe('structured: steps to reproduce, expected, actual'),
  priority: z.enum(['low', 'medium', 'high', 'critical']),
  suggestedTools: z.array(ToolSuggestionSchema).optional().default([]),
  acceptanceCriteria: z.string().optional()
});
```
Add `'parseBugReport'` to AIFeature union.

In `apps/api/src/ai/aiConfig.ts`: add config `parseBugReport: { maxTokens: 2048, cacheTTLMs: 0, retryOnValidationFailure: true }`

In `apps/api/src/ai/promptBuilder.ts`: add `buildParseBugReportPrompt(data: { bugReport, projectName, projectDescription? })` — instruct AI to extract title, structured description (steps/expected/actual), severity-based priority, acceptance criteria for verifying the fix. Wrap bugReport in `<user_input>` tags, truncate to 2000 chars.

In `apps/api/src/ai/aiService.ts`: add `parseBugReport(apiKey, data)` calling `callAndParse`.

In `apps/api/src/graphql/typedefs/ai.ts`: add mutation `parseBugReport(projectId: ID!, bugReport: String!): Task!`

In `apps/api/src/graphql/resolvers/ai.ts`: add resolver — validate project access, call AI, create task in DB with status 'todo' and taskType 'task', return created task.

In `apps/web/src/components/BugReportModal.tsx`: Modal with large textarea (placeholder: 'Paste bug report, error log, or user feedback...'), Submit button, loading state. On success: toast + close.

In `apps/web/src/pages/ProjectDetail.tsx`: add 'Bug' button in toolbar that opens BugReportModal.

In `apps/web/src/hooks/useProjectData.ts`: add `handleParseBugReport(bugReport: string)` that calls the mutation and adds returned task to state.

**FEATURE 3: PRD → Task breakdown**

In `apps/api/src/ai/aiTypes.ts`:
```typescript
export const PRDTaskSchema = z.object({
  title: z.string(),
  description: z.string(),
  priority: z.enum(['low', 'medium', 'high', 'critical']),
  estimatedHours: z.number().optional(),
  acceptanceCriteria: z.string().optional()
});
export const PRDEpicSchema = z.object({
  title: z.string(),
  description: z.string(),
  tasks: z.array(PRDTaskSchema)
});
export const PRDBreakdownSchema = z.object({
  epics: z.array(PRDEpicSchema)
});
```
Add `'breakdownPRD'` to AIFeature union.

In `apps/api/src/ai/aiConfig.ts`: add `breakdownPRD: { maxTokens: 4096, cacheTTLMs: 0, retryOnValidationFailure: true }`

In `apps/api/src/ai/promptBuilder.ts`: add `buildPRDBreakdownPrompt(data: { prd, projectName, projectDescription? })` — instruct AI to break PRD into epics each containing implementation tasks. Wrap prd in `<user_input>` tags, truncate to 4000 chars.

In `apps/api/src/ai/aiService.ts`: add `breakdownPRD(apiKey, data)`.

In `apps/api/src/graphql/typedefs/ai.ts`: add types `PRDEpic`, `PRDTask`, `PRDBreakdown` and mutations `previewPRDBreakdown(projectId: ID!, prd: String!): PRDBreakdown!` and `commitPRDBreakdown(projectId: ID!, epics: String!): [Task!]!` (epics as JSON string input for simplicity).

In `apps/api/src/graphql/resolvers/ai.ts`:
- `previewPRDBreakdown`: call AI, return preview
- `commitPRDBreakdown`: parse epics JSON, create epic tasks with taskType 'epic', create child tasks with parentTaskId pointing to epic, return all created tasks

In `apps/web/src/components/PRDBreakdownModal.tsx`: Modal with large textarea for PRD text, 'Analyze' button (calls preview), preview showing epics with nested task lists (expandable), 'Create Tasks' button (calls commit), loading states throughout.

In `apps/web/src/pages/ProjectDetail.tsx`: add 'PRD' button in toolbar.

In `apps/web/src/hooks/useProjectData.ts`: add `handlePreviewPRD(prd)` and `handleCommitPRD(epics)` functions with state for `prdPreview` and `prdLoading`.

**Verification:** `pnpm typecheck` passes for both packages.
acceptanceCriteria: Task dedup: existing titles injected into prompt, post-gen filter removes duplicates
Dedup applied to generateTaskPlan, previewTaskPlan, and expandTask
parseBugReport mutation creates structured task from bug report text
BugReportModal with textarea, submit, loading state, toast on success
PRD breakdown: preview/commit pattern with epics containing nested tasks
PRDBreakdownModal with textarea, preview with expandable epics, commit button
Both new modals accessible from ProjectDetail toolbar
All Zod schemas validate AI output
pnpm typecheck passes for both packages

tasks.title: Sprint transition analyzer + GitHub repo bootstrap — AI features with full UI
tasks.description:
Implement two AI features end-to-end: sprint transition analysis and repo-to-project bootstrapping.

**FEATURE 1: Sprint transition analyzer**

In `apps/api/src/ai/aiTypes.ts`:
```typescript
export const SprintTransitionTaskSchema = z.object({
  taskId: z.string(),
  reason: z.string()
});
export const SprintTransitionSchema = z.object({
  summary: z.string(),
  carryOver: z.array(SprintTransitionTaskSchema),
  deprioritize: z.array(SprintTransitionTaskSchema),
  recommendations: z.array(z.string())
});
```
Add `'analyzeSprintTransition'` to AIFeature union.

In `apps/api/src/ai/aiConfig.ts`: add `analyzeSprintTransition: { maxTokens: 2048, cacheTTLMs: 0, retryOnValidationFailure: true }`

In `apps/api/src/ai/promptBuilder.ts`: add `buildSprintTransitionPrompt(data: { sprintName, sprintGoal?, tasks: Array<{taskId, title, status, priority, assignee?, storyPoints?}>, completionRate })` — instruct AI to analyze incomplete tasks, recommend carry-over (high priority, in progress, nearly done) vs deprioritize (low priority, not started, scope creep), provide recommendations for next sprint.

In `apps/api/src/ai/aiService.ts`: add `analyzeSprintTransition(apiKey, data)`.

In `apps/api/src/graphql/typedefs/ai.ts`: add types `TransitionTask { taskId: ID!, reason: String! }`, `SprintTransitionAnalysis { summary, carryOver, deprioritize, recommendations }` and query `analyzeSprintTransition(sprintId: ID!): SprintTransitionAnalysis!`.

In `apps/api/src/graphql/resolvers/ai.ts`: add resolver — fetch sprint + its tasks (include assignee email), calculate completion rate (done / total), call AI, return analysis. Note: does NOT auto-move tasks — just recommends.

In `apps/web/src/components/SprintTransitionModal.tsx`: Modal showing:
- Summary text at top
- 'Carry Over' section: list of tasks with title + reason, checkbox to select for carry-over
- 'Deprioritize' section: list of tasks with title + reason, checkbox to deselect
- 'Recommendations' section: bullet list
- 'Apply' button that moves selected carry-over tasks to the next sprint and deselected deprioritize tasks to backlog (via existing updateTask mutations)
- Loading state while analyzing

Integrate into the sprint close flow — when user clicks 'Close Sprint' in BacklogView, show this analysis before confirming.

**FEATURE 2: GitHub repo → Project bootstrap**

In `apps/api/src/ai/aiTypes.ts`:
```typescript
export const RepoBootstrapSchema = z.object({
  projectDescription: z.string(),
  tasks: z.array(z.object({
    title: z.string(),
    description: z.string(),
    priority: z.enum(['low', 'medium', 'high', 'critical']),
    estimatedHours: z.number().optional(),
    taskType: z.enum(['epic', 'story', 'task']).optional().default('task')
  }))
});
```
Add `'bootstrapFromRepo'` to AIFeature union.

In `apps/api/src/ai/aiConfig.ts`: add `bootstrapFromRepo: { maxTokens: 4096, cacheTTLMs: 0, retryOnValidationFailure: true }`

In `apps/api/src/ai/promptBuilder.ts`: add `buildRepoBootstrapPrompt(data: { repoName, repoDescription?, readme?, packageJson?, fileTree: Array<{path, language?, size?}>, languages: string[] })` — instruct AI to analyze the repo structure and generate a project description + initial task breakdown for improving/maintaining the codebase. Truncate readme to 2000 chars, fileTree to 50 entries.

In `apps/api/src/ai/aiService.ts`: add `bootstrapFromRepo(apiKey, data)`.

In `apps/api/src/graphql/typedefs/ai.ts`: add `bootstrapProjectFromRepo(projectId: ID!): [Task!]!` mutation.

In `apps/api/src/graphql/resolvers/ai.ts`: add resolver —
- Fetch project + repo info (must have GitHub repo connected)
- Fetch file tree via `fetchProjectFileTree`
- Fetch README content via `fetchFileContent(installationId, owner, repo, 'README.md')`
- Fetch package.json content similarly (if exists)
- Call `bootstrapFromRepo(apiKey, { repoName, repoDescription, readme, packageJson, fileTree, languages })`
- Update project.description from AI result if currently empty
- Create tasks in DB from result
- Return created tasks

In `apps/web/src/pages/ProjectDetail.tsx`: add 'Bootstrap from Repo' button (only shown if project has a linked GitHub repo and has < 5 tasks). On click, show confirmation dialog ('Analyze linked repo and generate initial tasks?'), then call mutation, show loading, toast on success.

**Verification:** `pnpm typecheck` passes for both packages.
acceptanceCriteria: SprintTransitionSchema validates carry-over/deprioritize/recommendations output
analyzeSprintTransition query fetches sprint data and returns AI analysis
SprintTransitionModal shows carry-over and deprioritize lists with apply action
RepoBootstrapSchema validates project description + task array output
bootstrapProjectFromRepo fetches README + package.json + file tree from GitHub
Bootstrap creates tasks in DB, updates project description if empty
Bootstrap button only shown when repo connected and < 5 tasks
All Zod schemas validate AI output
pnpm typecheck passes for both packages

tasks.title: Outgoing webhooks — full stack with HMAC signing and management UI
tasks.description:
Add outgoing webhook support: fire HTTP requests on task/sprint/comment events with HMAC-signed payloads.

**1. Prisma schema — create `apps/api/prisma/schema/webhook.prisma`:**
```prisma
model WebhookEndpoint {
  id          String   @id @default(uuid())
  orgId       String   @map("org_id")
  url         String
  secret      String   // HMAC signing secret (plain, not encrypted — low sensitivity)
  events      String   // JSON string array: ["task.created", "task.updated", "sprint.closed", ...]
  enabled     Boolean  @default(true)
  description String?
  createdAt   DateTime @default(now()) @map("created_at")
  lastError   String?  @map("last_error")
  lastFiredAt DateTime? @map("last_fired_at")

  org Org @relation(fields: [orgId], references: [orgId], onDelete: Cascade)

  @@index([orgId, enabled])
  @@map("webhook_endpoints")
}
```
Add relation to Org model in `apps/api/prisma/schema/org.prisma`: `webhookEndpoints WebhookEndpoint[]`

**2. Migration SQL.**

**3. Create webhook dispatch service (`apps/api/src/utils/webhookDispatcher.ts`):**
```typescript
import crypto from 'crypto';

interface WebhookPayload {
  event: string;
  timestamp: string;
  data: Record<string, unknown>;
}

export async function dispatchWebhooks(
  prisma: PrismaClient,
  orgId: string,
  event: string,
  data: Record<string, unknown>
): Promise<void>
```
- Fetch enabled endpoints for org where `events` JSON array includes this event
- For each endpoint:
  - Build payload: `{ event, timestamp: new Date().toISOString(), data }`
  - Sign with HMAC-SHA256: `crypto.createHmac('sha256', endpoint.secret).update(JSON.stringify(payload)).digest('hex')`
  - Send POST with headers: `Content-Type: application/json`, `X-Webhook-Signature: sha256={signature}`, `X-Webhook-Event: {event}`
  - 5 second timeout
  - On success: update `lastFiredAt`
  - On error: update `lastError` with message, log warning
- Fire-and-forget: use `Promise.allSettled`, don't block the caller
- Supported events: `task.created`, `task.updated`, `task.deleted`, `sprint.created`, `sprint.closed`, `comment.created`

**4. GraphQL typeDefs — create `apps/api/src/graphql/typedefs/webhook.ts`:**
```graphql
type WebhookEndpoint {
  id: ID!
  url: String!
  events: String!  # JSON array
  enabled: Boolean!
  description: String
  lastError: String
  lastFiredAt: String
  createdAt: String!
}

webhookEndpoints: [WebhookEndpoint!]!
createWebhookEndpoint(url: String!, events: [String!]!, description: String): WebhookEndpoint!
updateWebhookEndpoint(id: ID!, url: String, events: [String!], enabled: Boolean, description: String): WebhookEndpoint!
deleteWebhookEndpoint(id: ID!): Boolean!
testWebhookEndpoint(id: ID!): Boolean!
```

**5. Update schema.ts** to import webhook typeDefs.

**6. Create resolver (`apps/api/src/graphql/resolvers/webhook.ts`):**
- CRUD operations: require org:admin
- `createWebhookEndpoint`: generate random secret (`crypto.randomBytes(32).toString('hex')`), validate URL format, validate events array against supported list
- `testWebhookEndpoint`: send a test payload `{ event: 'test', timestamp, data: { message: 'Webhook test from TaskToad' } }`, return true/false

**7. Update resolver index** to include webhook resolvers.

**8. Wire dispatch into mutations:**
- `apps/api/src/graphql/resolvers/task.ts`: after `createTask` → `dispatchWebhooks(prisma, orgId, 'task.created', { task })`; after `updateTask` → `dispatchWebhooks(prisma, orgId, 'task.updated', { task, changes })`
- `apps/api/src/graphql/resolvers/sprint.ts`: after `closeSprint` → `dispatchWebhooks(prisma, orgId, 'sprint.closed', { sprint })`

**9. Create WebhookSettings component (`apps/web/src/components/WebhookSettings.tsx`):**
- Fetch `webhookEndpoints` on mount
- List of endpoints: URL, events (pill badges), enabled toggle, last fired, last error (red text)
- Add form: URL input, event checkboxes (task.created, task.updated, sprint.closed, comment.created), description, Save
- Show generated secret (once, on creation) with copy button
- Test button per endpoint
- Delete button with confirmation

**10. Wire into OrgSettings (`apps/web/src/pages/OrgSettings.tsx`):**
Add 'Webhooks' section after the GitHub section, render WebhookSettings component.

**Verification:** `npx prisma validate` passes, `pnpm typecheck` passes for both packages.
acceptanceCriteria: WebhookEndpoint model with url, secret, events JSON, enabled flag, error tracking
HMAC-SHA256 signed payloads with X-Webhook-Signature header
Dispatch is fire-and-forget with 5s timeout
CRUD mutations for webhook endpoints with org:admin requirement
Test endpoint sends test payload and returns success/failure
Webhooks fired on task.created, task.updated, sprint.closed
WebhookSettings UI with endpoint list, add form, test button, event checkboxes
Secret shown once on creation with copy button
Integrated into OrgSettings
npx prisma validate passes
pnpm typecheck passes for both packages

You MUST run npx prisma generate in your worktree after adding the new schema file so TypeScript knows about the new model. Also fix implicit any types on ep/endpoint params. Additionally, task-006 SSE code looks good but cannot be merged until webhook types are fixed. Rebase onto main (task-001 and task-003 were merged) before resubmitting.

tasks.title: Contextual project chat — AI Q&A grounded in live project data
tasks.description:
Add a conversational AI chat feature that answers questions about the project using live task/sprint/activity data as context.

**1. Zod schema (apps/api/src/ai/aiTypes.ts):**
Add:
```typescript
export const ProjectChatResponseSchema = z.object({
  answer: z.string(),
  references: z.array(z.object({
    type: z.enum(["task", "sprint", "activity"]),
    id: z.string(),
    title: z.string()
  })).optional().default([])
});
export type ProjectChatResponse = z.infer<typeof ProjectChatResponseSchema>;
```
Add 'projectChat' to AIFeature union.

**2. Config (apps/api/src/ai/aiConfig.ts):**
Add: projectChat: { maxTokens: 2048, cacheTTLMs: 0, retryOnValidationFailure: true }

**3. Prompt builder (apps/api/src/ai/promptBuilder.ts):**
Add buildProjectChatPrompt(data: { question, projectName, projectDescription?, tasks: Array<{taskId, title, status, priority, assignee?, sprintName?}>, sprints: Array<{name, isActive, taskCount}>, recentActivity: Array<{action, field, taskTitle?, createdAt}>, knowledgeBase? })
- System: SYSTEM_JSON
- Instruct: answer the user's question about this project using ONLY the provided data. Include references to specific tasks/sprints mentioned in your answer. If the data doesn't contain enough info, say so.
- Truncate tasks to 50, activity to 20, wrap question in <user_input> tags

**4. Service (apps/api/src/ai/aiService.ts):**
Add projectChat(apiKey, data) calling callAndParse with ProjectChatResponseSchema.

**5. Export from apps/api/src/ai/index.ts.**

**6. GraphQL (apps/api/src/graphql/typedefs/ai.ts):**
Add types:
```graphql
type ChatReference { type: String!, id: ID!, title: String! }
type ProjectChatResponse { answer: String!, references: [ChatReference!]! }
```
Add query: projectChat(projectId: ID!, question: String!): ProjectChatResponse!

**7. Resolver (apps/api/src/graphql/resolvers/ai.ts):**
- Fetch project, tasks (top 50 non-archived with sprint+assignee info), sprints, recent activities (last 20)
- Build context object, call AI service
- Return answer + references

**8. Create ProjectChatPanel (apps/web/src/components/ProjectChatPanel.tsx):**
A slide-out panel or modal with:
- Chat message history (local state, not persisted)
- Input field at bottom with send button
- Each AI response shows the answer text + clickable reference pills (task/sprint links)
- Loading state while AI responds
- Clear chat button
- Scrollable message area

**9. Create useProjectChat hook (apps/web/src/hooks/useProjectChat.ts):**
- State: messages array, loading boolean
- sendMessage(question): calls projectChat query, appends Q+A to messages
- clearChat(): resets messages

**Verification:** pnpm typecheck passes for both packages.

tasks.title: Repo drift analysis + batch code generation — AI features with full UI
tasks.description:
Implement two AI features: repo-task drift detection and batch code generation for multiple tasks.

**FEATURE 1: Repo ↔ Task drift analysis**

In apps/api/src/ai/aiTypes.ts:
```typescript
export const DriftAnalysisSchema = z.object({
  summary: z.string(),
  outdatedTasks: z.array(z.object({ taskId: z.string(), title: z.string(), reason: z.string() })),
  untrackedWork: z.array(z.object({ description: z.string(), suggestedTaskTitle: z.string() })),
  completedButOpen: z.array(z.object({ taskId: z.string(), title: z.string(), evidence: z.string() }))
});
export type DriftAnalysis = z.infer<typeof DriftAnalysisSchema>;
```
Add 'analyzeRepoDrift' to AIFeature union.

In aiConfig.ts: add analyzeRepoDrift: { maxTokens: 4096, cacheTTLMs: 0, retryOnValidationFailure: true }

In promptBuilder.ts: add buildRepoDriftPrompt(data: { repoName, recentCommits: Array<{sha, message, date}>, openPRs: Array<{title, state}>, tasks: Array<{taskId, title, status, description?}>, fileChanges: string[] })
- Instruct AI to compare repo activity against task list: find tasks that are done in code but still open, find repo work with no matching task, find tasks that reference outdated code
- Truncate commits to 30, PRs to 10, tasks to 50

In aiService.ts: add analyzeRepoDrift(apiKey, data).

In typedefs/ai.ts: add types DriftOutdatedTask, DriftUntrackedWork, DriftCompletedButOpen, DriftAnalysis and query analyzeRepoDrift(projectId: ID!): DriftAnalysis!

In resolvers/ai.ts:
- Fetch project + repo info
- Fetch recent commits (last 30) via GitHub API — use existing githubCommitService or add a listRecentCommits function to githubFileService.ts. If no such function exists, use the GitHub REST API: GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/commits?per_page=30
- Fetch open PRs via GitHub API
- Fetch project tasks
- Call AI, return analysis

NOTE: You may need to add a helper to fetch recent commits from GitHub. If githubCommitService.ts or githubFileService.ts doesn't have this, add it to apps/api/src/github/githubFileService.ts (this file is safe — worker-3 doesn't touch it).

**FEATURE 2: Batch code generation**

In aiTypes.ts: add BatchCodeGenerationSchema wrapping multiple CodeGeneration results:
```typescript
export const BatchCodeGenerationSchema = z.object({
  files: z.array(GeneratedFileSchema),
  summary: z.string(),
  estimatedTokensUsed: z.number().optional()
});
```
Add 'batchGenerateCode' to AIFeature union.

In aiConfig.ts: add batchGenerateCode: { maxTokens: 8192, cacheTTLMs: 0, retryOnValidationFailure: true }

In promptBuilder.ts: add buildBatchCodeGenerationPrompt(data: { tasks: Array<{title, description, instructions}>, projectName, projectDescription?, existingFiles?, styleGuide?, knowledgeBase? })
- Instruct AI to generate code for ALL provided tasks in a single response, organizing files logically
- Truncate each task's instructions to 500 chars, cap at 5 tasks

In aiService.ts: add batchGenerateCode(apiKey, data).

In typedefs/ai.ts: add mutation batchGenerateCode(projectId: ID!, taskIds: [ID!]!, styleGuide: String): CodeGeneration!

In resolvers/ai.ts:
- Validate all tasks belong to project and have instructions
- Fetch project repo file tree if available
- Call AI, return combined CodeGeneration
- User can then create a single PR with all generated files via existing createPullRequestFromTask flow

Create apps/web/src/components/DriftAnalysisModal.tsx:
- Three sections: Outdated Tasks (with task links), Untracked Work (with "Create Task" buttons), Completed But Open (with "Mark Done" buttons)
- Loading state, summary at top
- Only shown when project has linked repo

Create apps/web/src/components/BatchCodeGenModal.tsx:
- Task selection: checkboxes for tasks with instructions
- "Generate Code" button calls batchGenerateCode
- Shows combined CodePreviewModal-style file list
- "Create PR" button for all files at once

**Verification:** pnpm typecheck passes for both packages.
acceptanceCriteria: DriftAnalysisSchema validates outdated/untracked/completed-but-open output
analyzeRepoDrift fetches commits + PRs + tasks and returns AI analysis
DriftAnalysisModal with three sections and action buttons
BatchCodeGenerationSchema validates multi-task code output
batchGenerateCode accepts multiple taskIds and returns combined files
BatchCodeGenModal with task selection and combined preview
GitHub commit/PR fetching works for drift analysis
pnpm typecheck passes for both packages

tasks.title: Split useProjectData into focused hooks + extract GraphQL queries
tasks.description:
Decompose the god-object useProjectData hook (~1150 lines, 37 useState, 40+ functions) into focused, single-responsibility hooks. Also extract inline GraphQL query strings.

**1. Create `apps/web/src/api/queries.ts`:**
Extract ALL inline GraphQL query/mutation strings from useProjectData.ts into typed constants:
```typescript
export const PROJECT_QUERY = `query Project($projectId: ID!) { project(projectId: $projectId) { ... } }`;
export const TASKS_QUERY = `query Tasks($projectId: ID!) { tasks(projectId: $projectId) { ...TASK_FIELDS } }`;
export const SPRINTS_QUERY = `...`;
export const CREATE_TASK_MUTATION = `...`;
export const UPDATE_TASK_MUTATION = `...`;
// ... all other queries and mutations used in useProjectData
```
Use the existing TASK_FIELDS constant from taskHelpers.ts via string interpolation.

**2. Create `apps/web/src/hooks/useSprintManagement.ts`:**
Extract sprint-related state and operations from useProjectData:
- State: sprints, activeSprint (computed)
- Functions: handleCreateSprint, handleUpdateSprint, handleDeleteSprint, handleCloseSprint, loadSprints
- Import queries from queries.ts
- Accept projectId and orgId as params
- Return sprint state + operations

**3. Create `apps/web/src/hooks/useAIGeneration.ts`:**
Extract all AI-related state and operations:
- State: isGenerating, previewTasks, previewLoading, committing, generatingInstructions, generatingCode, generatedCode, prdPreview, prdLoading
- Functions: openPreview, handleCommitPlan, handleGenerateInstructions, handleGenerateCode, handleRegenerateFile, handleSummarize, handleParseBugReport, handlePreviewPRD, handleCommitPRD
- Accept projectId, project (for knowledgeBase), and a taskRefreshCallback as params
- Return AI state + operations

**4. Create `apps/web/src/hooks/useTaskCRUD.ts`:**
Extract task CRUD operations:
- State: tasks, selectedTask, editingTitle, editTitleValue, err
- Functions: loadTasks, handleAddTask, handleUpdateTask, handleDeleteTask, handleSelectTask, handleStartEditTitle, handleTitleChange, handleTitleSave, handleBulkCreateTasks
- Accept projectId and orgId as params
- Return task state + CRUD operations
- Wrap setState dispatchers in action functions (don't expose raw setSelectedTask, setErr)

**5. Refactor `apps/web/src/hooks/useProjectData.ts`:**
Reduce to a thin orchestration layer that composes the focused hooks:
```typescript
export function useProjectData(projectId: string) {
  const project = useProject(projectId);  // just project fetch + updateProject
  const tasks = useTaskCRUD(projectId, project.data?.orgId);
  const sprints = useSprintManagement(projectId, project.data?.orgId);
  const ai = useAIGeneration(projectId, project.data, tasks.refresh);
  const toast = useToast();
  // ... compose and return unified interface for backwards compatibility
}
```
The return type should match the existing interface so ProjectDetail.tsx and other consumers don't need changes.

**6. Add useMemo/useCallback throughout the new hooks:**
- Wrap all handler functions in useCallback with proper dependency arrays
- Memoize computed values (activeSprint, rootTasks) with useMemo
- This is the main performance win — previously 37 useState + 40 functions all recreated on every render

**Key constraint:** The public API of useProjectData must remain backwards-compatible. ProjectDetail.tsx and other consumers should not need changes. The refactor is internal.

**Verification:** `pnpm --filter web typecheck` passes. The app behaves identically (no functional changes).
acceptanceCriteria: queries.ts contains all GraphQL strings extracted from useProjectData
useSprintManagement handles all sprint state and operations
useAIGeneration handles all AI state and operations
useTaskCRUD handles all task CRUD state and operations
useProjectData reduced to thin composition layer (<150 lines)
Public API of useProjectData unchanged (backwards compatible)
All handlers wrapped in useCallback, computed values in useMemo
pnpm --filter web typecheck passes
